name: Shrimp with Lobster Sauce #2
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Peanut or vegetable oil, divided
2 Packages Instant Chicken Broth and Seasoning Mix,
1 tablespoon Black Bean Sauce or Oyster Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Shell and devein shrimp, leaving tail "feathers" on.
2. In a large glass or stainless-steel bowl, combine 1 tablespoon cornstarch with the sherry, water, and salt, mixing to dissolve the cornstarch. Add shrimp and toss to coat. Let stand at room temperature for 20 minutes.
3. Heat 1 tablespoon oil over high heat in a 12-inch nonstick skillet or wok. Add shrimp and sherry mixture and cook, stirring quickly and frequently, until shrimp barely turn pink. Remove shrimp from the pan and set aside.
4. In the same pan, heat remaining oil; add garlic and ginger and stir-fry briefly (BE CAREFUL NOT TO BURN). Add pork and stir to combine; add 3/4 cup scallions, all but 2 tablespoons dissolved broth mix, sugar, and black bean (or oyster) sauce. Bring to a boil.
5. Reduce heat to low and return shrimp to the pan.
6. In a separate bowl or cup, dissolve remaining tablespoon cornstarch in remaining 2 tablespoons dissolved broth mix. Add to shrimp mixture and stir constantly. Return mixture to a boil. Reduce heat and let simmer for 1 minute.
7. Gradually stir in eggs, stirring just until eggs have set. Remove from heat.
8. Arrange hot rice on serving plate. Spoon shrimp mixture onto rice and sprinkle with remaining 1/4 cup scallions.
